article_title: PNNL Opens the First Prismatic Battery Cell Line at a US National Lab, Targeting Sodium-Ion and LFP Grid Storage

content_preview.title: PNNL Opens the First Prismatic Battery Cell Line at a US National Lab, Targeting Sodium-Ion and LFP Grid Storage
content_preview.summary: The DOE's Grid Storage Launchpad has commissioned the first prismatic battery cell production line at a US national laboratory, starting with sodium-ion and LFP chemistries.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

The U.S. Department of Energy's Grid Storage Launchpad has commissioned what it describes as the first prismatic battery cell production line at a U.S. national laboratory, according to [Pacific Northwest National Laboratory](https://www.pnnl.gov/news-media/new-prismatic-line-unlocks-powerful-possibilities-us-battery-research). The line, housed at PNNL in Richland, Washington, will begin by producing and evaluating two chemistries aimed at grid-scale storage: sodium-ion and lithium-...

editor_notes.content_quality: Strong, well-organized News piece (713 words) on PNNL's new prismatic cell line and the Grid Storage Launchpad. Clear sectioning and appropriate technical context.
editor_notes.source_verification: All 5 snapshots read from disk (HTTP 200). source-0 (PNNL) confirms '16 pieces of equipment', the '1400-square-foot' lab, 'A first for the national laboratories', the February testing, Mark Weller (principal investigator) and Adam Jivelekas, and every Weller/Jivelekas quote verbatim. source-1 (Interesting Engineering) confirms the nine-volt resemblance, the 93,000-square-foot facility, and the stacking/overheating/cylindrical comparison. source-3 (PNNL Inside GSL) confirms the Sprenkle 'all-in-one research facility' quote, 'two dozen', and the 100 kW / 400 kWh test capability. source-4 (energy.gov) confirms the $75 million facility and 30 research laboratories. source-2 (Mirage News) confirms the DOE Office of Electricity funding.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All specifics trace to snapshots. The article renders the lab area as '1,400-square-foot' where PNNL writes '1400-square-foot' — identical value, thousands-separator formatting only; not a discrepancy. All three speakers' quotes are verbatim and correctly attributed. Both internal cross-reference slugs (2026-03/19 sodium-ion grid pilot; 2026-04/29 CATL-HyperStrong) were verified to exist.
editor_notes.originality: New topic — frames itself as a follow-up in the ongoing sodium-ion/grid-storage thread and cross-references prior coverage rather than re-narrating it.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: High-quality, fully-sourced submission with verified cross-references. APPROVE.
